---
# ! Render via `targets::tar_make()` (the `render_manuscript` target in
# ! _targets.R), not `quarto render` directly - see manuscript.qmd.
title: "Continuous integration: rebuilding the pipeline on every push"
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(here)
here::i_am("manuscript/supplementary/ci-pipeline.qmd")
```

{{< include _back-to-manuscript.qmd >}}

::: {.callout-tip}
# The Short Version
- `.github/workflows/render.yml` runs `targets::tar_make()` on every push
  and PR - a fresh clone rebuilds the pipeline and renders html/docx/typst
  from scratch, on a machine that isn't anyone's local setup.
- No `DESCRIPTION` file exists (this isn't an R package), so R packages
  are listed explicitly in the workflow and must be kept in sync with
  `runme.R`'s `required_pkgs` by hand - there's no single source of truth
  for the package list.
- `r-lib/actions/setup-r-dependencies` also installs the Ubuntu system
  libraries these packages need (libcurl, libmagick++, Cairo, ...) via
  `pak`'s sysreqs database - plain `install.packages()` on a bare runner
  would fail on several of these without it.
- CI installs `rsvg-convert`, closing the one gap this template could
  never verify locally (this development environment has no
  Word/LibreOffice or `rsvg-convert` - see
  [images-mre.qmd](images-mre.qmd)'s SVG section) - CI's docx render is
  clean where the local one has an unavoidable warning.
- Rendered output uploads as a workflow artifact even on failure, since a
  partial `output/` (some formats rendered, one didn't) is itself the
  useful debugging signal for the "one bad format blocks the others"
  gotcha documented in CLAUDE.md.
- **A real first run hung for ~3 hours at `render_manuscript` and had to
  be cancelled by hand** - see "A real CI failure" below. `timeout-minutes:
  15` now caps that, and an explicit `quarto install chrome-headless-shell`
  step is a hypothesis fix for the actual cause, not yet confirmed working.
:::

# Why this notebook exists

Every bug this template's other notebooks document
([rendering-pipeline.qmd](rendering-pipeline.qmd),
[tables-mre.qmd](tables-mre.qmd), [images-mre.qmd](images-mre.qmd)) was
found by rendering locally and noticing something wrong - docx corruption,
a typst error, stale output. None of that requires a human to remember to
check all three formats after every change; a CI job does it automatically
and would have caught several of these regressions the moment they were
introduced, not whenever someone next happened to render typst.

# What the workflow actually does

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-ci-flowchart
%%| fig-cap: "One push, one clean-room rebuild of the whole pipeline"
flowchart TD
    A[push / pull_request] --> B["checkout"]
    B --> C["setup-r + setup-r-dependencies<br/>(installs R packages + Ubuntu sysreqs)"]
    C --> D["quarto-actions/setup<br/>(installs Quarto CLI)"]
    D --> E["quarto install chrome-headless-shell"]
    E --> F["apt-get install librsvg2-bin"]
    F --> G["testthat::test_dir()"]
    G --> H["source data-raw/import-data.R"]
    H --> I["targets::tar_make()"]
    I --> J["upload manuscript/output/ as an artifact"]
```

Each step, and why it's there:

1. **`actions/checkout`** - the usual first step, nothing template-specific.
2. **`r-lib/actions/setup-r` + `setup-r-dependencies`** - installs R itself,
   then the packages this template needs. Because there's no
   `DESCRIPTION` file, `setup-r-dependencies` takes an explicit `packages:`
   list rather than reading it from a manifest - this list has to be
   maintained by hand alongside `runme.R`'s `required_pkgs`, and the two
   can drift if one is updated without the other.
3. **`quarto-dev/quarto-actions/setup`** - installs the Quarto CLI itself,
   separately from R. Pandoc and the typst compiler both come bundled with
   Quarto (see [rendering-pipeline.qmd](rendering-pipeline.qmd)), so no
   further install is needed for either.
4. **`quarto install chrome-headless-shell`** - added after a real CI hang;
   see "A real CI failure" below for why this exists and what's actually
   confirmed about it.
5. **`librsvg2-bin`** - closes the SVG→docx gap documented in
   [images-mre.qmd](images-mre.qmd): without `rsvg-convert` on `PATH`,
   pandoc embeds a raw SVG in docx with no raster fallback. This
   development environment never had it installed, so that gap was always
   "unverified-risky" locally; CI is the first place it's actually closed.
6. **`testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat", stop_on_failure = TRUE)`** -
   runs before the pipeline build, so a broken `detect_render_format()` or
   export helper fails fast with a clear test name instead of surfacing as
   a confusing render error further down. See
   [testing-mre.qmd](testing-mre.qmd) for the test setup itself.
7. **`source("data-raw/import-data.R")`** - a fresh runner has no
   `data/` folder (git-ignored), so the pipeline's `import_data` target has
   nothing to read until this runs once. Mirrors what `runme.R` does for a
   first-time local setup (see
   [`data-raw/data-raw-readme.md`](../../data-raw/data-raw-readme.md)).
8. **`targets::tar_make()`** - builds every target and renders `manuscript/`
   in all three formats, exactly as it would locally.
9. **`actions/upload-artifact`**, with `if: always()` - runs even if
   `tar_make()` fails partway through, so a partial `manuscript/output/`
   (e.g. html/docx present, typst missing after a hard failure) is
   downloadable from the workflow run for debugging, not just a red X with
   no further detail.

# A real CI failure

The first actual run of this workflow did not behave like the local
testing that motivated it. Tests passed (14/14), `import-data.R` ran, and
`tar_make()` progressed cleanly through `example_data_file`, `import_data`,
`calculate_model`, `fig_01_example`, `tbl_01_example`, and
`export_figures` - then hung at `render_manuscript dispatched` for
**~3 hours** before someone cancelled it by hand. GitHub Actions' default
job timeout is 6 hours, so nothing would have stopped it on its own.

The workflow's own cleanup log named the culprit: orphaned `chrome`,
`chrome_crashpad_handler`, and `deno` processes, terminated only because
the job was cancelled. `render_manuscript` is the one step that renders
`manuscript/`'s mermaid diagrams
([images-mre.qmd](images-mre.qmd), [rendering-pipeline.qmd](rendering-pipeline.qmd))
to PNG for docx/typst, and Quarto does that via a Deno-managed headless
Chrome - confirmed real (not a guess) by running
`quarto install chrome-headless-shell` directly in this session: it's a
genuine, separate ~115MB download, distinct from the Quarto CLI itself.
Locally (Windows), that download/launch had apparently already happened
transparently and fast on some earlier run and was never noticed as a
distinct step; on a fresh Ubuntu runner with nothing cached, it's the
most likely thing to hang - either a slow/stuck download, or a launch
failure specific to the runner's environment (headless Chrome commonly
needs system libraries or sandbox flags a minimal image may not have).

**What was done about it, and what's still unconfirmed:**

- `timeout-minutes: 15` on the job (local renders take ~4 minutes) - this
  part is certain: whatever happens next, it can't silently run for hours
  again.
- An explicit `quarto install chrome-headless-shell --no-prompt` step
  before the render, so this exact download happens as its own named,
  visible step (with output and a bounded time budget) instead of lazily
  triggering deep inside `tar_make()` with no separate log to read. This
  is a **hypothesis fix, not a confirmed one** - the download itself was
  tested and works, but whether pre-installing it this way actually avoids
  whatever specifically hung in the real CI run hasn't been observed. If
  the next run still hangs or fails at this step (or still at
  `render_manuscript`), that's the next thing to dig into - likely missing
  system libraries for headless Chrome on this exact runner image, which
  would need identifying from that run's own log rather than guessed at
  here.

# What this doesn't do

No caching of R package installs or the `_targets/`/`_freeze/` stores
across runs - every run is a genuinely clean rebuild, which is the point
(it's what actually exercises the "does this work from a fresh clone"
question), but it also means every run pays the full install + render
cost. If render time becomes a problem, `actions/cache` keyed on the
package list and lockfile would be the next thing to add - not done here
to keep this workflow's failure modes easy to reason about while the
template is still this new.

**Where this actually stands:** the real CI run above confirms
`setup-r-dependencies`' package/sysreqs resolution, the test step, and
`import-data.R` all work on a genuine Ubuntu runner - that part is no
longer a guess. `render_manuscript` specifically is not yet confirmed
working in CI; the `chrome-headless-shell` pre-install is this session's
best-supported hypothesis for why it hung, tested only to the extent that
the download itself works, not that it fixes the hang. Push again and
check the Actions tab before trusting the full pipeline; treat
`render_manuscript` in CI as "should work now" rather than "confirmed
working."

# References
